name: List
goal: Render N homogeneous items in a vertical sequence with consistent spacing and optional dividers.
description: Generic flat list container with configurable dividers, density, and optional interactive hover states. Suitable for notifications, search results, settings rows, and audit logs.
limitations: ::slotted(*) only reaches direct children; wrapping items in an extra element will prevent divider and density styles from applying. No semantic list role is applied — consumers who need role=list must add it themselves and ensure children carry role=listitem. Data-driven declarative rendering (data attribute) is deferred to v2; v1 is slot-only.

props.name: dividers
props.description: Controls the divider between items. hair = 1px soft border; line = 1px strong border; none = gap-only spacing.

props.name: density
props.description: Controls padding-block on each slotted item. comfortable > cozy > compact.

props.name: interactive
props.description: Adds hover background and focus-visible outline to slotted items. Visual hint only — consumers provide real interactivity via anchors or buttons inside items.

slots.description: The list items — any element is accepted. Items must be direct children for divider and density styles to apply.

cssVariables.name: --ce-state-hover
cssVariables.description: Hover background on slotted items when interactive is set. Falls back to --ce-surface-2.

cssVariables.name: --ce-surface-2
cssVariables.description: Fallback hover background when --ce-state-hover is not defined.

cssVariables.name: --ce-color-blue
cssVariables.description: Focus-visible outline color when interactive is set.

cssVariables.name: --ce-transition
cssVariables.description: Transition timing for hover background animation when interactive.

a11y.notes: No role is applied to the host. role=list would require every direct child to be a listitem, but ce-list accepts unrestricted slot content. Consumers who know their content is a semantic list should add role=list to the ce-list element and role=listitem to each child. The interactive attribute adds visual hover/focus styles but does not imply clickability — real interactivity (anchors, buttons) must be provided by consumers inside each item.
